suggested_title: Sneh Patel - MD Student (Pathways Program) at Harvard Medical School
prospect_notes: Sneh Patel is an MD student in the Pathways program at Harvard Medical School, having graduated from Emory University with a degree in Neuroscience and Behavioral Biology. Originally from India, he immigrated to the US as a teenager. After graduating from Emory, he conducted research at the National Cancer Institute's Pediatric Oncology Branch under the mentorship of Dr. Andrea Gross, then joined the NIH NIAID as a post-baccalaureate fellow before starting at HMS. He was selected as a 2024 Goldwater Scholar. His research interests broadly include developing multiomic computational methods to improve risk stratification and precision cancer care, with a focus on pediatric oncology and cancer predisposition genes. At HMS, he currently works in the Gillani Lab at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute as an Alex Miliotis Fellow, conducting research on pathogenic germline variants in early-onset pediatric cancer.

suggested_title: Megan Bishop - MS Bioethics Student at Harvard Medical School Center for Bioethics
prospect_notes: Megan Bishop is an Emory University alumna who graduated with a degree in Neuroscience and Behavioral Biology and a minor in Neuroethics, and is now pursuing a Master of Science in Bioethics at Harvard Medical School's Center for Bioethics. Her time at Emory, particularly under the mentorship of professor Gillian Hue, Ph.D., sparked her interest in how science, medicine, and ethics intersect in research and patient care. During her time at Emory she conducted thesis research in the Berman Lab using neuronal and mechanical models to analyze the biophysics of social communication during Drosophila courtship song. She is interested in exploring ethical challenges shaping healthcare, with a focus on equity, autonomy, and justice in medicine. Her undergraduate background in neuroethics and neuroscience positions her at the intersection of biomedicine and bioethics research.

suggested_title: Dominique Jones - Graduate Student, MS Media Medicine and Health at Harvard Medical School
prospect_notes: Dominique Jones graduated with highest honors from Emory University and is now pursuing a Master of Science in Media, Medicine, and Health at Harvard Medical School's Department of Global Health and Social Medicine. The HMS MS in Media, Medicine, and Health is a nine-month program and the only US master's degree program offering evidence-based, multidisciplinary storytelling with an arts-driven curriculum focused on health interventions. Her research interests include maternal and fetal medicine, sexual and reproductive health, birth outcomes, health disparities, and public health. She moved to Boston from Atlanta to pursue this graduate program after excelling at Emory. Her focus on health communication and reproductive equity makes her a strong candidate interested in health-related media and policy work.

_enrichment.title: Graduate Student, MS Media Medicine and Health
